fix: My Ads owner-only instant React rendering
root_causes: The compiled React dashboard rendered a hardcoded My Ads count of 3 and a hardcoded Views count of 892.
The React My Ads array was permanently initialized as an empty array, so the native list stayed blank.
A separate runtime script tried to replace React-owned children with innerHTML after the API response, creating a React/DOM race.
The API ownership compatibility queries were executed serially, increasing wait time on legacy schemas.
changes: React now hydrates the current user's My Ads synchronously from the v3 cache and migrates the existing v2 cache.
React subscribes to authenticated ehemehe:dashboard-ads-updated events and renders only rows owned by the current user.
Dashboard counts are calculated from the same real owner rows; the bundled 3 and 892 values were removed.
Direct /dashboard/ads navigation opens the My Ads tab immediately.
The legacy DOM painter is disabled whenever React owns the My Ads mount.
Edit and Delete controls receive the real ad ID after React rendering.
Supabase custom_fields.owner_user_id and user_id ownership queries run in parallel and remain server-side filtered.
Loading and settled-empty states are visible instead of a blank page.
production_note: The automated suite validates the packaged code with mocked authenticated ownership responses. A real production Supabase session can only be observed after deployment.
